summary: Iteration 149 - Customer Payment (Receive Payments) Page Left Sidebar Panel Testing. All features tested and working correctly. Backend API GET /api/customers/receivables-summary returns customer list with balance, overdue_balance, invoice_count fields. Frontend PaymentsPage.js has left sidebar panel similar to PaySupplierPage.js with filter, sort, and search functionality.

minor.issue: Backend returns customers unsorted - frontend handles sorting. Not a bug, just a note.

issues: Console warning: 'In HTML, <tr> cannot be a child of <span>' - React hydration warning from ScrollArea component wrapping table rows. This is a minor React rendering issue that doesn't affect functionality.

critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/customers/receivables-summary endpoint correctly aggregates invoice data per customer with balance, overdue_balance, invoice_count, overdue_count fields
include_zero=true parameter correctly returns all customers including zero-balance ones
PaymentsPage.js left sidebar panel correctly implements customer list with data-testid attributes
Filter toggle (With Balance / All) works correctly - 36 customers with balance, 64 total
Sort toggle (by Balance / by Name) works correctly
Search field filters customer list by name
Clicking customer row selects it and loads invoices in right panel
Selected customer shows highlighted with green left border (border-l-[#1A4D2E])
Customer name appears in 'Received From' field when selected
Customer Balance displays in header when customer is selected
Outstanding Transactions table loads with invoices for selected customer
Clear button (X) on Received From field deselects customer
DUE badges appear for customers with overdue amounts
Total receivables amount (₱151,521.94) shown at top of customer list
test_credentials: Super Admin: janmarkeahig@gmail.com / Aa@58798546521325, Manager PIN: 521325
context_for_next_testing_agent: PaymentsPage left sidebar panel feature complete and tested. Similar to PaySupplierPage pattern. Backend endpoint /api/customers/receivables-summary is in customers.py router (not accounting.py) because the customers router has a {customer_id} catch-all route that would intercept it otherwise.

tests_performed.test: Backend: Default excludes zero balance customers

tests_performed.details: No error when branch_id parameter provided
All customers have valid overdue_balance values

tests_performed.test: Frontend: PaymentsPage loads with left sidebar panel

tests_performed.details: Clicking customer row selects it, name appears in 'Received From' field

tests_performed.test: Frontend: Customer balance display in header
Frontend: Filter toggle - 'All' shows more customers
tests_performed.details: 64 customers shown with 'All' filter vs 36 with 'With Balance'
Both 'Sort by Name' and 'Sort by Balance' buttons clickable
Cash, Check, Bank Transfer, GCash, Maya method buttons found

tests_performed.test: Frontend: Payment amount auto-applies to invoices

40 DUE badges found on customers with overdue amounts
₱151,521.94 total shown at top of customer list
